expectations.text: Handles inline spec without needing Confluence fetch
expectations.evidence: Section 2 explicitly states: 'Skip the Confluence fetch entirely. The spec content is already available in the conversation. Proceed directly to Step 2: Analyze Spec.'

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs issue-types FRONTEND to check available types
Maps requirement #3 (mobile Safari bug) to Bug type
expectations.evidence: Section 4 table maps #3 'Fix profile changes not saving on mobile Safari' to Bug type. Section 7 Step 2 uses '--type Bug'.

expectations.text: Maps requirement #6 (API docs) to Task type
expectations.evidence: Section 4 table maps #6 'Update API documentation for new profile endpoints' to Task type. Section 7 Step 7 uses '--type Task'.

expectations.text: Maps feature requirements (#1,2,4,5) to Story type
expectations.evidence: Section 4 table maps #1 (avatar upload), #2 (bio field), #4 (completion indicator), #5 (social media) all to Story type.

expectations.text: Creates Epic first, then child tickets with --parent
expectations.evidence: Section 7 Step 1 creates the Epic first, captures 'FRONTEND-200', then Steps 2-7 all use '--parent FRONTEND-200'.

expectations.text: Presents plan with labeled issue types before creating
expectations.evidence: Section 6 shows a complete breakdown table with Type column (Epic, Story, Bug, Task) for all items, before the create commands in Section 7.
